title: lagophylla glandulosa
intro: Lagophylla glandulosa, also known as the sticky bush, is a small shrub native to Western Australia, characterized by its sticky leaves and small yellow flowers. The medicinal properties of Lagophylla glandulosa include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and even alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Lagophylla glandulosa can be made into teas, infusions, and topical creams, making it a versatile plant for natural remedies.
